summary: Phase 14 — Activity Timeline + Cross-Role Visibility. All 20 new backend tests pass plus 23 Phase 12/13 regression tests (43/43, 100%). All key Phase 14 UI testids verified across Admin/Client/Specialist/Operator with full schedule-proposal, timeline modal and non-conformity flag flows.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is 3261 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into modules (routers/auth, routers/requests, routers/admin, routers/operator, routers/twin, services/activity_log).
log_event swallows all exceptions silently (warning only). Acceptable for non-critical telemetry but consider a metrics counter so silent failures are observable in admin.
GET /api/admin/activity-stream limit param is not capped on the server side — recommend enforcing a server-side max (e.g. 200) to prevent accidental large payloads.
Non-conformity flag endpoint validates target existence well; consider adding an idempotency key or dedupe window to avoid duplicate flags from impatient operators (UX improvement, not a bug).
test_credentials: client@propmanage.io/Client123!, specialist@propmanage.io/Spec123! (active_view restored to specialist), admin@propmanage.io/Admin123!, operator@propmanage.io/Op123!
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_Phase14_* requests, one TEST_Phase14_E2E property + twin + full job lifecycle, several open and one resolved nonconformity, and one TEST_Phase14 NC flag from operator UI. Demo data only — no production data modified. Test client `test_p14_*@test.io` accounts also created during RBAC + E2E flows.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live in /app/backend/tests/test_phase14.py (uses BASE_URL from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, no defaults). Operator dashboard appears to default to twins view on load — operator-tab-twins testid may be on a sub-nav rather than primary bottom nav; flag-twin-* buttons render directly without needing that click. specialist's accept now goes through ScheduleProposalModal (NOT direct API call), so any older test that posts /accept directly is still valid but the UI flow requires opening modal first. Activity stream returns events ordered DESC; timeline events on /api/requests/{id}/timeline return ASC. RBAC: random new client gets 403 on timeline, admin/operator(who validated twin)/owner client/assigned specialist get 200.
rca of the issue: No issues observed. log_event helper is correctly invoked at all 12 instrumented points; schedule_proposal payload is correctly persisted on accept events and retrievable via timeline endpoint.
